query: TSMC A14 process node volume production timeline specifications performance improvement N2 comparison
a_issues: Content A contains conflicting timeline information. The official TSMC A14 page snippet says 'A14 production is on track for 2028,' while the TSMC press release snippet says 'Volume production is scheduled for 2027' and 'risk production in first quarter of 2026.' The 2027 volume-production claim appears inconsistent with the anchor/official page indicating 2028. A also includes third-party snippets that repeat 2028 and performance numbers with slight variation ('close to 20%' density gain vs official 'more than 20%'), which adds some inconsistency though less severe than the timeline conflict.
b_issues: Content B also lacks a clean official timeline. The official TSMC A14 page result is largely unusable boilerplate and does not expose the key A14 facts. The TSMC press release snippet provides the N2 comparison numbers accurately, but not the A14 volume-production date. Some third-party results mention yield progress and N2 timing, but there is no clear official A14 production timeline in the snippets. B therefore omits an important part of the question rather than answering it directly. It also includes a potentially noisy third-party result about N2/A14 production timing.
reasoning: Both sets contain enough reliable information to answer the specifications/performance-vs-N2 part: the official TSMC press release in both says A14 offers up to 15% speed improvement at the same power, or up to 30% power reduction at the same speed, with more than 20% higher logic density versus N2. For the production timeline, A has the needed fact from an official TSMC page ('on track for 2028') but also includes a contradictory official-appearing press-release snippet claiming 2027 volume production, which could mislead the model. B avoids that direct contradiction but fails to surface the official A14 timeline at all because its TSMC A14 page snippet is mostly boilerplate and the press release snippet shown does not include the production date. Since A has the fact but contradicts itself, and B is cleaner on specs but incomplete on timeline, neither clearly enables a correct full answer better than the other.
